questions.question: Each vowel in the word 'EQUIPMENT' is changed to the following letter in the English alphabetical order and each consonant is changed to the preceding letter in the English alphabetical order between the letters in the English alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical order between the letter which is the second from the right end and the fourth from the left end in the group of letters thus formed?
questions.explanation: EQUIPMENT After changing the vowel to the following letter and consonant to the preceding letter: EQUIPMENT E+1Q-1U+1I+1P-1M-1E+1N-1T-1 FPVJOLFMS 'J' is the fourth letter from the left end and 'M' is the second from the right end. J K L M Two letters are between J and M.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark(?) in the given series? 185 ? 227 269 325
questions.explanation: Pattern of the series- 185 + 14 = 199 199 + 28 = 227 227 + 42 = 269 269 + 56 = 325 199 will replace the question mark (?).

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs that can sequentially replace the * signs and balance the equation. 18 * 45 * 100 * 24 * 36 * 52
In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two signs. 34 X 2 - 102 ÷ 24 + 3 = -26
questions.explanation: On interchanging ÷ and + signs we get, LHS = 34 X 2 - 102 + 24 ÷ 3 = 34 X 2 - 102 + 8 = 68 - 102 + 8 = -26

questions.question: Three of the following four-letter clusters are alike in a certain way and one is different. Pick the odd one out.
questions.explanation: There is a gap of 5 letters between the first and the last letter. Also there is a gap of 5 letters between the second and third letter except IDKM.

questions.question: Select the correct mirror image of the given combination when the mirror is placed at MN as show.
questions.explanation: Figure (II) is the correct mirror image of the given combination.

questions.question: Select the option in which the given figure is embedded (rotation is NOT allowed).
questions.explanation: The given figure is embedded in the option figure III.

questions.question: Select the option which is related to the first term in the same way as the fourth term is related to the third term and the sixth term is related to the fifth term. SXWM : ? : : HURC : CHRU : : VJYF : FJVY
questions.explanation: Here, the term is arranged according to alphabetical order. HURC == CHRU. VJYF == FJVY. Similarly, SXWM == MSWX.

questions.question: In a certain code language, A Ω B means 'A is the wife of B' A + B means 'A is the brother of B' A B means 'A is the father of B' A ∆ B means 'A is the son's daughter of B' Based on the above, how is I related to T if 'I Ω J F + G ∆ T'?
questions.explanation: I Ω J F + G ∆ T I Ω J means 'I is the wife of J' J F means 'J is the father of F' F + G means 'F is the brother of G' G ∆ T means 'G is the son's daughter of T' I is the daughter-in-law of T.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth letter cluster in the same way as the second letter-cluster is related to the first letter-cluster and the fourth letter-cluster is related to the third letter-cluster. DRUM : HITN :: AKOY : EPNB :: GIRL : ?
questions.explanation: A (1)B (2)C (3)D (4)E (5)F (6)G (7)H (8)I (9)J (10)K (11)L (12)M (13) Z (26)Y (25)X (24)W (23)V (22)U (21)T (20)S (19)R (18)Q (17)P (16)O (15)N (14) D + 4 = H R = Opposite of R = I U - 1 = T M = Opposite of M = N A + 4 = E K = Opposite of K = P O - 1 = N Y = Opposite of Y = B Similarly, G + 4 = K I = Opposite of I = R R - 1 = Q L = Opposite of L = O Thus, the answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters which, when sequentially placed from left to right in the blanks below, will complete the letter series. M _ O _ W _ N O _ X M _ _ P Y _ N _ P _
options: N P M P N O M O Z
O V M N P X N P Y
N O P W M P M O Z
M P X O N N N O Y
questions.explanation: Option (a): M N O P W M N O P X M N O P Y M N O P Z Thus, the series 'MNOP' is repeating 4 times. Also, W + 1 = X X + 1 = Y Y + 1 = Z So, option (a) is correct. Option (b): M O O V W M N O N X M P X P Y N N P P Y There is no pattern. So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c): M N O O W P N O W X M M P P Y M N O P Z There is no pattern. So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d): M M O P W X N O O X M N N P Y N N O P Y There is no pattern. So, option (d) is incorrect. Thus, the answer is option (a).

questions.question: Introducing a man, a woman said, "He is the husband of my mother-in-law's only son's daughter". How is that man related to a woman?
questions.explanation: Introducing a man, a woman said, "He is the husband of my mother-in-law's only son's daughter". That man is the son-in-law of a woman.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'PADLOCKS' is coded as 'RJBNMEBQ', then how will 'NOTARIZE' be coded as in the same language?
questions.explanation: The word is first reversed: PADLOCKS - SKCOLDAP Now, subtracting 1 position to the first four letters and adding 1 position to the last four letters. SKCOLDAP = RJBNMEBQ Similarly, NOTARIZE : EZIRATON = DYHQBUPO

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. 23 : 72 :: 38 : 117 :: 46 : ?
Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark(?) in the given series? 400, 8, 80, 40, 16, 200, 3.2, ?
questions.explanation: Here, the alternate numbers are considered. 400 ÷ 5 = 80 8 X 5 = 40 80 ÷ 5 = 16 40 X 5 = 200 16 ÷ 5 = 3.2 200 X 5 = 1000 Thus, the answer is option (b).

questions.question: Which letter-cluster will replace the question mark(?) to complete the given series? HXLC, JVKE, LTJG, NRII, PPHK, RNGM, ?
The position of how many letters will remain unchanged if each letter in the word DECLINE is arranged in reverse alphabetical order i.e., starting from the last letter (Z).
questions.explanation: DECLINE NLIEEDC So, the position of no letter will remain unchanged.

questions.question: In the following question, which one set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? b _ a b _ c _ b b c a _ b _ a _
questions.explanation: b c a b/ b c a b/ b c a b/ b c a b

questions.question: On 30th April 1996 it was Tuesday. What was the day of the week on 30th April 1995?
questions.explanation: The year 1996 is a leap year. It has 2 odd days. Thus, the day of 30th April 1995 is 2 days before the day of 30th April 1996. Tuesday - 2 = Sunday Hence, on 30th April 1995, it will be Sunday.

questions.question: In the question below, two statements are given followed by conclusions: I, II and III. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read the conclusions and decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some pools are tubs. All tubs are trunks. Conclusions: I. All trunks are tubs. II. All tubs are pools. III. Some trunks are pools.
options: None of I, II and III follow
questions.explanation: Some pools are tubs. All tubs are trunks. All trunks may be tubs but it is not definitely true. All tubs may be pools but it is not definitely true. So, conclusions I and II do not follow. Some trunks are pools. So, only conclusion III follows.

questions.question: If '-' means 'x', 'x' means '+', '+' means '÷', '÷' means '-' , then the value of 24+4- 5÷10x7 is
questions.explanation: We have: 24+4- 5÷10x7 After interchanging signs, we get: 24 ÷ 4 x 5- 10 +7 = 30- 10 +7 = 27

questions.question: Choose a figure which would most closely resemble the unfolded form of the last figure which shows the manner in which the folded paper has been cut.
questions.explanation: Figure (III) is the correct unfolded figure.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. (The words must be considered as meaningful English word and must not be related to each other based on the number of letter/consonants/vowels in the word). Attractive : Ugly :: Quick : ?
questions.explanation: As Attractive is the antonym of Ugly similarly Quick is the antonym of Sluggish. So, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Arrange the words given below in a meaningful sequence. 1. Irrigation 2. Storage 3. Fertilization 4. Sowing 5. Harvesting
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: 43152. Sowing, fertilization, irrigation, harvesting, Storage.

questions.question: Select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: 82/2 = 41 and 41 + 5 = 46 All the pairs follow this rule except 94-51. 94/2 = 47 and 47 + 5 = 52

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Olympic archery events: i. Both men and women compete over the same standard distance of 70 metres in recurve archery. ii. Mixed team archery consists of one male and one female archer representing the same nation. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are factually accurate about Olympic archery regulations. The Olympic archery distance is 70 meters for both men's and women's recurve archery. In Olympic archery, the target is set at a distance of 70 meters (approximately 76.5 yards) from the shooting line for both men and women competing in the recurve discipline. A mixed team consists of two archers, one man and one woman and a team consists of three archers of the same gender from the same nation.

questions.question: Perini Sivatandavam or Perini Thandavam of ________ is a typical form of dance that belongs to the warrior clan of 11th century AD.
questions.explanation: Perini Sivatandavam or Perini Thandavam of Telangana is a typical form of dance that belongs to the warrior clan of 11th century AD. This is actually a male dance form performed in the Shiva Temple as an invocation to the Lord Rudra. The 11th century rulers of Kakatiya, Ganapathi Deva, Pratapa Rudra Deva of Warangal and Rani Rudrama Devi were all worshippers of Lord Shiva and tremendously helped the warrior dance form. This art, after the fall of Kakatiyas, disappeared from the scene due to lack of patronage.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Assertion (A): Laterite soil is not suitable for cultivation. Reason (R): These soils are poor in organic matter, nitrogen, phosphate and calcium. Select the correct answer from the options given below.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.explanation: Laterite soils are poor in organic matter, nitrogen, phosphate and calcium, while iron oxide and potash are in excess. Hence, laterites are not suitable for cultivation. The laterite soils develop in areas with high temperature and high rainfall. With rain, lime and silica are leached away, and soils rich in iron oxide and aluminium compound are left behind. Humus content of the soil is removed fast by bacteria that thrives well in high temperatures. Laterite soils are widely cut as bricks for use in house construction.

questions.question: Identify which of the following expenditure comes under the Revenue expenditure of the Government? I. Interest payments II. Subsidies III. Investment in shares IV. Pensions Answer Codes:
questions.explanation: Revenue Expenditure is expenditure incurred for purposes other than the creation of physical or financial assets of the Central Government. It relates to those expenses incurred for the normal functioning of the Government departments and various services, interest payments on debt incurred by the Government, and grants given to State Governments and other parties (even though some of the grants may be meant for the creation of assets). It includes interest payments, defence services, subsidies, salaries and pensions. Capital Expenditures are expenditures of the Government which result in the creation of physical or financial assets or reduction in financial liabilities. This includes expenditure on the acquisition of land, building, machinery, equipment, investment in shares, and loans and advances by the Central Government to State and Union Territory Governments.

questions.question: Consider the 'Permanent Settlement' (1793) introduced by Lord Cornwallis. Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?
options: The Zamindars were recognized as the owners of the land.
The amount of revenue to be paid to the Company was fixed permanently.
If the Zamindar failed to pay the revenue by sunset on the fixed day, his land was liable to be auctioned.
The settlement was primarily implemented in the Madras and Bombay Presidencies.
questions.explanation: Option d is incorrect because the Permanent Settlement was primarily implemented in Bengal, Bihar, and Odisha, whereas the Ryotwari system was the dominant system in the Madras and Bombay Presidencies. Explanation of Statements: a. is Correct. Under this settlement, the British East India Company recognized Zamindars as the legal owners of the land. They were transformed from mere revenue collectors into landlords, provided they paid the fixed revenue to the state. b. is Correct. As the name implies, the revenue demand was fixed permanently. The Company hoped that a fixed demand would encourage Zamindars to invest in the improvement of land, as any surplus income resulting from increased production would remain with them. c. is Correct. This was known as the "Sunset Law." The Company was extremely rigid about the timing of payments. If the revenue was not paid by the sunset of the specified date, the Zamindar's estate was liable to be confiscated and auctioned to recover the arrears. d. is Incorrect. The Permanent Settlement was implemented in the Bengal Presidency (modern-day West Bengal, Bangladesh, Bihar, and Odisha) and parts of Northern Madras. In contrast, the Ryotwari System (pioneered by Thomas Munro) was primarily implemented in the Madras and Bombay Presidencies, while the Mahalwari System was prevalent in the North-Western Provinces and Punjab. Further Insights: The Permanent Settlement was introduced by Lord Cornwallis in 1793. Impact on Peasants: While Zamindars became owners, the actual cultivators (peasants) were reduced to the status of tenants with no secure rights to the land, leading to widespread exploitation. Revenue Ratio: The revenue was fixed at a very high level initially-roughly 10/11th went to the Company and 1/11th was retained by the Zamindar. Absentee Landlordism: Many Zamindars lived in cities and left the revenue collection to agents, leading to a disconnect between the owners and the agricultural reality of the land.

questions.question: In October 2025, India's Priteesmita Bhoi created a new weightlifting world youth record by successfully lifting 92kg in the clean and jerk section of the girls' _________ category at the Asian Youth Games 2025 in Manama, Bahrain.
questions.explanation: India's Priteesmita Bhoi created a new weightlifting world youth record by successfully lifting 92kg in the clean and jerk section of the girls' 44kg category at the Asian Youth Games 2025 in Manama, Bahrain. The record mark earned her a gold medal in the clean and jerk section. She also clinched silver in the snatch section with a 66kg lift to finish with two medals. At the Asian Youth Games, weightlifting medals are awarded separately for the snatch and the clean and jerk. However, there are no medals for overall totals.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement(s). I. In November 2025, Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated a towering 77-foot bronze statue of Lord Rama. II. It is at the Shree Samsthan Gokarn Partagali Jeevottam Math in Goa. III. The statue itself, crafted by legendary sculptor Ram V Sutar, known for designing the Statue of Unity.
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated a towering 77-foot bronze statue of Lord Rama at the Shree Samsthan Gokarn Partagali Jeevottam Math in Goa. The unveiling comes as part of the Math's 550th anniversary celebrations, known as the Sardha Panchashatamanotsava, in which the Prime Minister is participating during his Goa visit. It is one of the oldest and most prominent Dvaita Vaishnava institutions in India. The ceremony marked a milestone moment for the math, which belongs to the Gaud Saraswat Brahmin community and has served as a central Vaishnava spiritual seat for centuries. Alongside the statue, PM Modi inaugurated the Ramayana Theme Park, released a special postal stamp and a commemorative coin. The statue itself, crafted by legendary sculptor Ram V Sutar, known for designing the Statue of Unity, has been presented as the world's tallest statue of Lord Ram. Modi described it as a symbol of unity, devotion and India's cultural continuity.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023: 1. The BNS has significantly streamlined the criminal code, reducing the total number of sections from 511 in the IPC to 358. 2. For the first time in India's general criminal law, "Community Service" has been introduced as a specific category of punishment for petty offences. 3. The colonial-era offence of "Sedition" (Section 124A of IPC) has been completely removed and has not been replaced by any provision related to acts against the State. 4. The BNS introduces a specific provision for "Mob Lynching," where a group of five or more persons committing murder on grounds of race, caste, or community can be punished with life imprisonment or death. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Only 1, 2, and 4 are correct. Statement 1 (Correct): The BNS is indeed more concise, featuring 358 sections compared to the 511 sections of the old Indian Penal Code (IPC). Statement 2 (Correct): Section 4 of the BNS introduces Community Service as a reformative punishment for minor crimes like defamation, misconduct by a drunken person, or small thefts (below ₹5,000) by first-time offenders. Statement 3 (Incorrect): While the word "Sedition" and Section 124A are gone, they have been replaced by Section 152 (Acts endangering sovereignty, unity, and integrity of India). The new law is actually broader, covering electronic communication and financial means used to incite subversive activities. Statement 4 (Correct): Under Section 103(2), the BNS specifically addresses Mob Lynching by a group of five or more, providing for the death penalty or life imprisonment-a distinction that did not exist in the IPC. Further Insights: Implementation: The BNS, along with the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Samhita (BNSS) and the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (BSA), came into legal effect on July 1, 2024. Terrorism Defined: The BNS provides a clear definition of "Terrorist Acts" (Section 113) within the general penal law for the first time, rather than relying solely on special laws like UAPA. Organized Crime: A new chapter has been added to deal with organized crime (Section 111), including kidnapping, robbery, and cyber-crimes committed by crime syndicates. Crimes against Women and Children: The BNS prioritizes these offences by placing them in the very first chapters of the code, increasing penalties for gang rape and introducing a specific offence for sexual intercourse by "deceitful means" (promise to marry).

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the central theme of the novel "Such a Long Journey" by Rohinton Mistry?
options: The impact of the Partition of India on urban middle-class families in West Bengal and how they bounced back
The moral dilemmas, political manipulation, and everyday struggles of an ordinary Parsi bank clerk set against the backdrop of 1970s Bombay
The decline of feudalism and rise of nationalism in colonial India and its impact on the zamindars
The experiences of Indians serving in the Navy during World War II
questions.explanation: The novel follows Gustad Noble, a dedicated Parsi bank clerk and family man living in a Parsi enclave in Bombay. His life is upended by a request from an old friend, Major Bilimoria, which draws him into a world of political corruption and money laundering. The story weaves Gustad's personal struggles-such as his son's refusal to attend IIT-with the larger political climate of Indira Gandhi's India and the 1971 Indo-Pak war. Further Insights: Literary Recognition: Such a Long Journey (published in 1991) was Rohinton Mistry's debut novel and was shortlisted for the prestigious Booker Prize. It also won the Commonwealth Writers Prize. The novel was adapted into a highly acclaimed film in 1998, starring Roshan Seth as Gustad Noble.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the powers and function of the Vice-President of India is/are correct? A. He acts as the ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha. B. He acts as President when a vacancy occurs in the office of the President due to his resignation, removal, death or otherwise. C. He can act as President only for a maximum period of twelve months within which a new President has to be elected. Select the code for the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.explanation: The functions of Vice-President are two-fold: 1) He acts as the ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha. In this capacity, his powers and functions are similar to those of the Speaker of Lok Sabha. In this respect, he resembles the American vice-president who also acts as the Chairman of the Senate - the Upper House of the American legislature. 2) He acts as President when a vacancy occurs in the office of the President due to his resignation, removal, death or otherwise. He can act as President only for a maximum period of six months within which a new President has to be elected.

questions.question: Read the statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct option: Assertion (A): The Human Development Index (HDI) captures only economic dimensions of development. Reason (R): HDI includes indicators related to income, health, and education to measure overall human well-being.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: A is false - HDI is a composite index covering income, health, and education, not just economic dimensions. R is true - it correctly describes the three dimensions of HDI. The index is based on the human development approach, developed by Mahbub ul-Haq, anchored in Amartya Sen's work on human capabilities, and often framed in terms of whether people are able to "be" and "do" desirable things in life. It was further used to measure a country's development by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)'s Human Development Report Office.

questions.question: Which of the following organisations was established in 1885?
questions.explanation: Indian National Congress was formed in the year 1885 in Mumbai. Its founders included A.O. Hume and Dadabhai Naoroji. The Communist Party of India (CPI) is the oldest communist party in India. The CPI was founded in modern-day Kanpur on 26 December 1925. Muslim League, political group that led the movement calling for a separate Muslim nation to be created at the time of the partition of British India (1947). The Muslim League was founded in 1906 to safeguard the rights of Indian Muslims. The East India Association was founded by Dadabhai Naoroji in 1866, in collaboration with Indians and retired British officials in London.

questions.question: In December 2025, in a landmark for gender representation in Indian higher education, veteran academic Dr Manali Kshirsagar took charge as the first full-time woman Vice-Chancellor of which state public university?
questions.explanation: Veteran academic Dr Manali Kshirsagar took over as the first full-time woman Vice-Chancellor of the Rashtrasant Tukadoji Maharaj Nagpur University and immediately listed her priorities, saying she would focus on launching technology-oriented courses. She took charge from the acting Vice-Chancellor Dr Madhavi Chavare. Addressing a press conference, Kshirsagar said she would focus on launching innovative technology-based courses to make students more employable.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding NAMASTE Scheme: I: It is scheme to eradicate unsafe sewer and septic-tank cleaning practices introduced by the Ministry of Jal Shakti. II: NAMASTE stands for "National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem" III: The schemes aims to stop deaths of Sewers and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs) and to promote mechanization of cleaning operations with a vision to reduce hazardous cleaning and ensure safety of sanitation workers. Select the correct answer using the codes below:
options: Only Statements I and III are correct.
Only Statements I and II are correct.
Only Statements II and III are correct.
questions.explanation: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MoSJE) has formulated a scheme "National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem" (NAMASTE). The process of extending the Scheme to all the Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) of the country has been initiated. Main features of the Scheme to be implemented in all ULBs are:- (i) NAMASTE envisages identifying the Sewer/Septic Tank Workers (SSWs). (ii) Occupational Training and distribution of PPE Kits to SSWs. (iii) Assistance for Safety Devices to Sanitation Response Units (SRUs). (iv) Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits to identified SSWs and their families under the Ayushman Bharat- Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY). (v) The Action Plan will promote mechanization and enterprise development by providing funding support and subsidy (capital +interest) to the sanitation workers, to procure sanitation related equipment. (vi) Massive campaigns would be undertaken jointly by the ULBs & NSKFDC to spread awareness about the interventions of NAMASTE.

questions.question: Who among the following was NOT a member of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was not directly elected by the people of India on the basis of adult franchise, the Assembly comprised representatives of all sections of the Indian society - Hindus, Muslims, Sikhs, Parsis, Anglo-Indians, Indian Christians, SCs, STs including women of all these sections. The Assembly included all important personalities of India at that time, with the exception of Mahatma Gandhi. Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha, the oldest member, was elected as the temporary President of the Constituent Assembly. Later, Dr. Rajendra Prasad was elected as the President of the Assembly. Similarly, both H.C. Mukherjee and V.T. Krishnamachari was elected as the Vice-Presidents of the Assembly.

questions.question: Identify the group of the periodic table to which cobalt belongs.
questions.explanation: Cobalt is a lustrous, very hard silvery metal belonging to a group called the "transition metals". It is one of only 3 ferromagnetic transition elements along with iron and nickel of Group 9 of the periodic table. Group 6: This group includes elements like Chromium (Cr), Molybdenum (Mo), Tungsten (W), and others. They are often referred to as Chalcogens. Group 9: This group includes elements like Cobalt (Co), Rhodium (Rh), Iridium (Ir), and others. They are often referred to as Cobalt Group. Group 5: This group includes elements like Vanadium (V), Niobium (Nb), Tantalum (Ta), and others. They are often referred to as Vanadium Group. Group 7: This group includes elements like Manganese (Mn), Technetium (Tc), Rhenium (Re), and others. They are often referred to as Manganese Group.

questions.question: ____________ Festival is an annual river festival to be celebrated in the state of Assam.
questions.explanation: The Dwijing Festival is an annual river festival of Assam state celebrated in the Bodoland Territorial Area Districts region. It is celebrated on the banks of Aie river near the Hagrama Bridge of Chirang District It is celebrated with great fervour and colourful events viz. ethnic trade and food festival, exhibition, games & sports, river campaign & adventure and various cultural programs to make it a centre of attraction for the visitors as a New Year Festival. The other festivals mentioned are associated with different regions: Sangai Festival: Celebrated in Manipur. Cherry Blossom Festival: Celebrated in Meghalaya. Hornbill Festival: Celebrated in Nagaland.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of bryophyte that lives in many environments and is characterised by its small, flattened leaves, root-like rhizoids, and peristome?
questions.explanation: Funaria is a genus of approximately 210 species of moss. Mosses are bryophytes that live in many environments and are characterized by their short flat leaves, root-like rhizoids, and peristomes. Small, flattened leaves: Mosses have leaf-like structures, but they are not true leaves as they lack vascular tissue. Root-like rhizoids: Mosses have rhizoids that anchor them to the substrate. These are not true roots as they don't absorb water and nutrients like roots. Peristome: This is a ring of tooth-like structures around the opening of the moss capsule (where spores are stored). The peristome helps in the dispersal of spores. Further insights: Ulothrix: This is a genus of filamentous green algae. Algae are not bryophytes. Cladophora: This is another type of green algae, also filamentous. Ulva: This is a genus of green algae, commonly known as sea lettuce. It has a sheet-like thallus.

questions.question: With reference to the Nehru's report of 1928, which of the statements is/are correct? A. Rejection of separate electorates B. Nineteen Fundamental Rights C. One third Muslim representation in the Central Legislature Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: All parties conference met in February 1928 and appointed a sub-committee under the chairmanship of Motilal Nehru to draft a constitution. The report was finalized by August 1928, with major recommendations of: dominion status, rejection of separate electorates, linguistic provinces, 19 fundamental rights and so on. Separate electorates and One-third Muslim representation in the central legislature are the parts of 14 points of Jinnah. (Spectrum's Modern India)

questions.question: Which of the following best describes India's 'Mission LiFE' initiative?
options: A scheme to provide subsidised solar panels to rural households
A global initiative promoting pro-planet individual behaviour and mindful consumption
A programme to expand India's forest cover by 25 million hectares
An initiative to develop electric vehicle charging infrastructure in tier-2 cities
questions.explanation: Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) is an India-led global mass movement introduced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at COP26 in Glasgow (2021) to mobilize individuals to become "Pro-Planet People". It promotes mindful consumption-rather than wasteful, 'use-and-dispose' habits-to protect the environment through daily, sustainable actions.

questions.question: Match the following: I. Revenue Deficit A. Total Revenue Expenditure-Total Revenue receipts-Grants for creation of capital assets II. Effective Revenue Deficit B. Total Revenue expenditure-Total Revenue Receipts III. Fiscal Deficit C. (Revenue expenditure + Capital Expenditure) - (Revenue Receipts + Capital receipts excluding borrowing) IV. Primary Deficit D. Fiscal Deficit - Interest Payments Select the correct option from the code given below:

Consider the following statements about the Product or Value-added method in income accounting. I. It is estimated by measuring the aggregate value of final goods and services produced by all the firms. II. Factor payments like the wages, interest, profits and rents paid are not included in this method. Which of the following is/ are correct?
questions.explanation: Product method is the measuring the aggregate value of final goods and services produced by all the firms. It considers only the value added to an item. The value added by a firm is Value of production of the firm minus the value of intermediate goods used. Hence, statement 1 is correct. The value added by the firm is distributed among its factors of production by means of wages, interests, profits and rents paid and adds up to value added of the firm. Hence, Statement 2 is incorrect.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about "Purana Qila" in Delhi: 1. Its construction was initiated by Jahangir. 2. It houses Qila-I-Kuhna Masjid and Sher Mandal. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Purana Quila was built between 1538 and 1545 by the Mughal emperor Humayun. The fort originally lay on the bank of the river Yamuna before the river changed its course. The Purana Quila was built by Humayun in an attempt to build a city of his own. The Qila-I-Kuhna Masjid was built by Sher Shah when he had captured the Purana Quila after defeating Humayun in 1541. The prayer hall is the most imposing part of the masjid that has five doorways with horse shoe shaped arches. The original plans of building the entire place with marble was jeopardized due to supply shortage. But the fusion of marble and sandstone has given the masjid a unique style. The Sher Mandal is now an observatory, octagonal in shape. The structure and style of the building suggests that the Sher mandal was built for entertainment purposes. The style and design of the building proves a definite coupling of the Muslim and Hindu architectural magnificence.

questions.question: The Tarnetar Mela is famous for its unique cultural traditions. Which of the following features does not represent the traditional identity of this fair?
options: Young men carrying exquisitely embroidered umbrellas (Chhatris)
Single men and women seeking matrimonial alliances
Performance of the traditional folk dance known as 'Hudo'
The mass immersion of idols in the holy Ganges River
questions.explanation: The Tarnetar Mela is held in Gujarat, near Trinetreshwar Temple in Surendranagar district. It is not related to the tradition of mass immersion of idols in the Ganges River, which is primarily seen in states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and West Bengal, especially during festivals like Durga Visarjan or Ganesh Visarjan. Further Insights: Tarnetar Mela is associated with the Trinetreshwar Mahadev Temple. The fair showcases Rabari, Bharwad, and Koli tribal traditions. Embroidered umbrellas (Chhatris) take months to craft and signify the groom's readiness. The fair includes camel rides, cattle trading, and cultural processions. It reflects the Solanki era tradition of community gatherings near temple tanks.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST): 1. The JWST operates primarily in the infrared spectrum, allowing it to see through cosmic dust clouds to observe the first stars and galaxies. 2. Unlike the Hubble Space Telescope, JWST orbits the Earth at a much higher altitude to avoid atmospheric interference. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is incorrect because the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) does not orbit the Earth; it orbits the Sun at the second Lagrange point (L2). Explanation of Statements: 1. is Correct. The JWST is designed as an infrared observatory. Because infrared light has longer wavelengths, it can pass through dense clouds of gas and dust that block visible light. This allows scientists to observe "stellar nurseries" (where stars are born) and detect the light from the very first galaxies that formed after the Big Bang, which has been stretched (redshifted) into the infrared spectrum over billions of years. 2. is Incorrect. This is a common misconception. While the Hubble Space Telescope orbits the Earth (at an altitude of about 550 km), the JWST does not orbit our planet. Instead, it orbits the Sun at a specific location called the Second Lagrange Point (L2), which is 1.5 million kilometers away from Earth. This location is chosen because it is gravitationally stable and allows the telescope to keep the Sun, Earth, and Moon on the same side of its sunshield, keeping its instruments extremely cold and dark. Further Insights: Comparison with Hubble: Hubble observes primarily in the visible and ultraviolet spectra. JWST's mirror is much larger (6.5 meters compared to Hubble's 2.4 meters), giving it significantly higher sensitivity. Thermal Management: To detect faint infrared signals, JWST must operate at temperatures below 50 Kelvin (-223C). This is achieved through its massive five-layer tennis-court-sized sunshield. International Collaboration: The mission is an international collaboration between NASA, the European Space Agency (ESA), and the Canadian Space Agency (CSA). Launch: JWST was launched on December 25, 2021, aboard an Ariane 5 rocket.

questions.question: There are 12 sides in the regular polygon. What is the maximum value of the exterior angle of a polygon?
questions.explanation: Number of sides = 12 Measure of each external angle = 360/12 = 30°

questions.question: What is the reflection of the point (4, -4) on the Y - axis?
questions.explanation: Since, the point (4, -4) is in the fourth quadrant and the reflection is on Y - axis. So, the reflection of this point will be in the third quadrant. Reflection of the point (4, -4) on the Y - axis = (-4, -4)

questions.question: If 5x - y = 5, and xy = 10_________9 , then the value of x3 - y3_________125 is:
There is a circle with centre B and AD and AC are two tangents to the circle such that ∠DAB = 30°. If radius of the circle is 18 cm, then what is the value of √3(AD + AC)?
questions.explanation: ∠DAB = ∠CAB = 30° In ∆ABC, tan 30° = CB/AC => 1/√3 = 18/AC => AC = 18√3 cm = AD Hence, √3(AD + AC) = √3 * 2 * 18√3 = 108

questions.question: Pratibha scored 80% marks in five subjects together viz., Hindi, Science, Maths, English, and Sanskrit, wherein the maximum marks of each subject were 105. She scored 89 marks in English, 92 marks in Hindi, 81 marks in Science and 98 marks in Sanskrit. Did she pass in the Maths subject and by how many marks if each subject had a pass mark of 65 marks?
questions.explanation: Maximum marks of the examination = 5 X 105 = 525 Pratibha's score = 525 X 80/100 = 420 Pratibha's score in Maths = 420 - (81 + 98 + 92 + 89) = 420 - 360 = 60 Therefore, she fails by 5 marks as the passing marks in each subject is 65.

questions.question: A container contains 40% acid in a mixture of acid and water. A part of this mixture is replaced by another mixture containing 25% acid. In the new mixture, the percentage of acid becomes 30%. What is the ratio in which these mixtures were mixed?
Rajan spends 10% of salary on rent, then 20% of the remainder on transport, then 30% on food, then 50% on bills. He deposits ₹4000 and keeps ₹2300. Find his salary.
questions.explanation: Let salary = 100x. After rent: 90x. After transport: 90x X 0.80 = 72x. After food: 72x X 0.70 = 50.4x. After bills: 50.4x X 0.50 = 25.2x remain. 25.2x = (4000 + 2300) = 6300 => x = 6300/25.2 = 250 Salary = 100x = 100 X 250 = Rs 25 000

questions.question: Which one of the following is the arithmetic mean of √6 - √5 and its reciprocal?
If 34 : 910.4 :: 9-4.4 : x, then find the value of x.

A shopkeeper has four kinds of rice 92 kg of type A rice, 115 kg of type B rice, 138 kg of type C rice and 69 kg of type D rice. He wants to store it into minimum number of containers of equal size without mixing. Find the size of the container and the number of containers required to do the needful.
questions.explanation: Size of the container = HCF of 92, 115, 138 and 69 = 23 And number of containers = 92/23 + 115/23 + 138/23 + 69/23 = 18

questions.question: Arjun and Binod enter a partnership with capitals in the ratio 7: 4. After 6 months, Binod withdraws one-third of his capital. After 8 months, Arjun increases his capital by 50%. If the total profit at year-end is ₹2,07,000, what is Binod's share?
questions.explanation: Let Arjun's and Binod's investments be Rs. 7x and Rs. 4x, respectively. Arjun's contribution * Time = 7x * 8 + 10.5x * 4 = 98x Binod's contribution * time = 4x * 6 + 8x/3 * 6 = 40x Profit ratio = 98x/40x = 49: 20 Hence, Binod's share = 20/69 X ₹207000 = ₹60000

questions.question: A solid toy is in the form of a hemisphere surmounted by a right circular cone. Height of the cone is 2cm and the diameter of the base is 4cm. If a right circular cylinder circumscribes the solid, find how much more space it will cover.
questions.explanation: Let BPC be the hemisphere and ABC be the cone standing on the base of the hemisphere. Radius BO of the hemisphere (as well as of the cone) = ½ * 4 = 2 cm Now, let right circular cylinder EFGH circumscribe the given solid, radius of the base of the right circular cylinder = HP = BO = 2 cm Height of the cylinder = AP = AO + OP = 2cm + 2cm = 4 cm Now, Volume of the right circular cylinder - Volume of the solid = (π * 22 * 4 - (2/3 * π * 23 + 1/3* π * 22 * 2)) cm3 = (16π - 8π) cm3 = 8π cm3

questions.question: If the average of ‘P’ numbers is ‘k’ and on adding a number ‘a’ to the ‘P’ numbers, the average become ‘L’. Then find the value of P?
questions.explanation: " Sum of P numbers = kP Sum of (P+1) numbers = (P+1)L kP + a = (P + 1) L kP – LP = L – a P(k−L) = L – a P = (L - a)/(k - L)

questions.question: Simplify the expression: sin 4x cos 4x_________sin2 4x - cos2 4x
questions.explanation: Note sin 4x · cos 4x = (1/2)·sin 8x Also, sin²4x - cos²4x = - (cos²4x - sin²4x) = - cos 8x Thus, expression = [(1/2)·sin 8x]/[ - cos 8x] = - (1/2)·[sin 8x/cos 8x] = - (tan 8x)/2

questions.question: A boy covers a total distance of 'd' km. He covers half of the distance at the speed of 20 km/h and after that increases his speed by 50%. Find the average speed of the boy.
questions.explanation: 1st half speed = 20 km/ h Second half speed = 20 X 150/100 = 30 km/h Average speed = 2 X S1 X S2 / (S1 + S2) = 2 X 20 X 30 / (20 + 30) km/h = 24 km/h

questions.question: A man, a woman, and a boy can complete a job in 10, 15, and 20 days respectively. When working together, how many boys must assist one man and one woman to complete the work in 3/5th of a day?
questions.explanation: Total work = LCM of 10, 15, and 20 = 60 units Efficiency of A man = 6 units per day Efficiency of a woman =4 units per day Efficiency of a boy = 3 units per day Let the required number of boys = X Therefore, (6 + 4 + 3X) * (3/5) = 60 10 + 3X = 100 3X = 90 X = 30

questions.question: Find the radius of the hemisphere (in cm) which is moulded from a cuboid of dimensions 49*33*12 cm3.
A sum becomes 4 times of itself in 12 years at a certain rate of simple interest per annum. In how much time it will become 11 times of itself at the same rate of simple interest?
questions.explanation: Let rate = R% The sum becomes 4 times of itself in 20 years. Then, 4 * sum - sum = sum * R * 12/100 R = 25 Let the sum becomes 11 times of itself at the 25% rate of simple interest in y years. Then, 11 * sum - sum = sum * 25 * y/100 y = 40 Express Method 4 times means simple interest is 3 times the sum. 11 times means simple interest is 10 times the sum. It took 12 years to be 3 times the sum, so it will take (12 x 10/3) = 40 years to be 10 times the sum.

questions.question: The base of a pyramid is a rectangle of length 8 cm. The width of rectangle is 2 cm less than its length. If the volume of pyramid is 320 cm3 then, the height of pyramid is:
questions.explanation: Length of rectangle = 8 cm Width of rectangle = 6 cm Area of base = L * W = 8 * 6 = 48 sq. cm Volume of pyramid = 320 (1/3) * base area * Height = 320 (1/3) * 48 * height = 320 Height = 320/16 = 20 cm

questions.question: Δ MNO is right angled at N. If MN = 2x - y, MO = 2x + y, NO = y, and P is a point on NO such that NP = (y - 2x). Find NP: PO
questions.explanation: By Pythagoras theorem,

questions.question: A trader sold two watches for Rs. 2700 each, neither losing nor gaining on the article. If he sold one of the watches at a loss of 10%, then another watch is sold at the profit of:
questions.explanation: Total Selling price = 2700 + 2700 = Rs.5400 10% loss = 1/10 or Selling price/Cost price = 9/10 Given, 9 units = Rs.2700 10 units = Rs.3000 The cost price of one article = Rs.3000 For neither losing nor gaining, the Cost price of the second article should be = 5400 - 3000= Rs.2400 Profit% = (2700 - 2400) x 100/2400 = 12.5%

questions.question: A child has launched a kite that soars H meters above the ground. The kite string, perfectly taut with no slack, is tied to a peg fixed in the ground. The string makes an angle of 60° with the ground, and its length is given as 11√3 meters. Which of the following statements is correct? (A) The value of H is perfect square. (B) The value of H lies between 30 and 40.
A sum of ₹8000 was lent partly at 7% and partly at 9% simple interest. If the total annual interest is ₹620, what is the ratio in which the money was lent at given rates?
A square lawn of side 60 m has a uniform gravel path running inside along its boundary. Gravelling costs Rs 29232 at Rs 63 per m2. Find the width of the path.
questions.explanation: Let x = width(m). Area of path (inside a square) = a2 − (a − 2x)2 => 4ax − 4x2, where a = 60. Paid area = 29232 ÷ 63 = 464 m2. So 240x − 4x2 = 464 => x2 − 60x + 116 = 0. Discriminant = 3600 − 116 X 4 = 3136 = 562 => x = (60 − 56)/2 = 2 m.

questions.question: Three circles of radius 7 cm are kept touching each other. The string is tightly tied around these three circles. What is the length of the string?
questions.explanation: r = 7 cm The length of the string = Length of string not in contact with circle + Length of string in contact with circle = (3 * 2r) + 3 * = (3 * 14) + 3 * = (42 + 14π) or (42 + 14p)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Serene
questions.explanation: Serene: calm, peaceful, and untroubled; tranquil. Agitated: feeling or appearing troubled or nervous. Anxious: worried. 'Calm' is the right synonym here. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. He said to me, "I will give you a good advice".
options: He told me that he will give me a good advice.
He told me that I would give him a good advice.
He told that he would give me a good advice.
He told me that he would give me a good advice.
questions.explanation: While changing direct speech into indirect speech, 'said to' is changed into 'told', 'to' is not used after 'told'. Use of the objective case of the appropriate pronoun is necessary after 'told'. Present Modals (e.g. Will, Can, May) are changed into Past Modals (i.e. Would, Could, Might). Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The scientist's discovery was considered a/an ________ moment in modern physics.
questions.explanation: The context of the sentence highlights the importance of a scientist's discovery in the field of modern physics. The blank requires an adjective [that also modifies the noun "moment"] that signifies great importance or long-term significance. The correct option is "historic", which means something that is important in history or has a lasting impact. It fits well with the idea of a groundbreaking scientific moment. The other options do not fit: "Delicate" implies fragility or sensitivity, not importance. "Slippery" is used to describe surfaces or situations that are difficult to hold or handle, which doesn't relate to discoveries. "Absolute" means total or complete, but doesn't convey importance or historic value. Thus, the correct sentence is: "The scientist's discovery was considered a historic moment in modern physics." Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Celerity
questions.explanation: Celerity - swiftness of movement; speed. Slowness - the quality of moving, acting, or happening at a low speed; a lack of speed. Since celerity means swiftness or speed, its direct opposite is the lack of speed, which is slowness. Hence, option a) is the correct answer. Speed - the rate at which someone or something moves; a synonym of celerity. Grace - smooth and elegant movement. Danger - the possibility of suffering harm or injury.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Mull over
options: To behave recklessly; to idly squander one's wealth
A structure, situation, or institution that is insubstantial, shaky, or in constant danger of collapse
To seek to gain favor by flattery or attention
To take a long time to make a decision
questions.explanation: The idiom 'Mull over' means 'To take a long time to make a decision'. Hence option d) is the correct answer.'Play ducks and drakes' means 'To behave recklessly; to idly squander one's wealth'. 'To curry favour' means 'To seek to gain favor by flattery or attention'. 'A house of cards' means 'A structure, situation, or institution that is insubstantial, shaky, or in constant danger of collapse'.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who believes that everything in the universe is God
questions.explanation: Pantheist: A person who believes that God is identical with the universe or that everything is divine. Fatalist: Someone who believes that all events are predetermined and inevitable. Agnostic: A person who believes that nothing is known or can be known about the existence of God. Deist: A person who believes in God but rejects revealed religion; believes God does not interfere with the universe. So, "a person who believes that everything in the universe is God" is best described as a "pantheist". Hence, option (d) is the answer.

questions.question: Spot the correct spelling for an operation that produces the same result whether it is performed once or multiple times.
questions.explanation: "Idempotent" refers to an operation that, when applied multiple times, yields the same result as when applied once. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the correct sentence in active voice from the following options.
options: Can the experts solve the problem?
Could the problem be solved by the experts?
Can the solving of the problem be done by the experts?
Solving the problem was accomplished by the experts.
questions.explanation: Option (a) is the correct answer. The question in Active voice is an interrogative sentence. The answer to this question will be either "yes" or "no". These types of sentences usually begin with a Modal Auxiliary verb. The format of such a sentence in Active voice is: Modal + Subject + V1 + Object? The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (a): Can the experts solve the problem?

questions.question: According to the passage, when can violence in movies serve a positive purpose?
options: When it replaces dialogue in storytelling
When it is used only for visual appeal
When it increases the length of the film
questions.explanation: The passage clearly states that although violence can have negative effects, it is not always harmful. It mentions that films often use violence to reflect real-life situations or to convey moral lessons, such as showing the consequences of crime and wrongdoing. In such cases, violence goes beyond entertainment and contributes to social awareness. Options (b), (c) and (d) are incorrect because they either reduce violence to mere visual appeal or mention aspects not discussed in the passage. Therefore, option (a) best captures when violence can serve a positive purpose. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: What does the passage suggest about the effect of violent movies on empathy?
options: It significantly increases empathy among viewers
It has no impact on emotional responses
It may reduce empathy towards others
It improves understanding of social relationships
questions.explanation: The passage explicitly mentions that "excessive consumption of violent media can increase aggressive thoughts and reduce empathy towards others." This indicates that frequent exposure to violent content can dull emotional sensitivity and make individuals less responsive to others' feelings. Options (a) and (d) are incorrect because the passage does not suggest any positive effect on empathy or relationships, while option (b) is wrong since the passage clearly states there is an impact. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the overall tone of the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage discusses both the negative and positive aspects of violence in movies. On one hand, it explains the harmful effects, such as desensitization to violence, increased aggression, and reduced empathy. On the other hand, it also acknowledges that violence in films can serve meaningful purposes, such as reflecting real-life situations, conveying moral lessons, and promoting social awareness. Since the author examines the issue from multiple perspectives in an objective and reasoned manner, the tone is balanced and analytical. Option (a) is incorrect because the passage is not entirely critical, option (b) is wrong because the author clearly presents an analysis rather than indifference, and option (d) is incorrect because the tone is serious, not humorous. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: What is the closest antonym of "desensitized" in the context of the passage?
questions.explanation: In the passage, "desensitized" means becoming less emotionally responsive or less affected by violence due to repeated exposure. Therefore, its opposite would be being emotionally responsive or aware, which is best captured by the word "sensitive." Options (a), (b), and (c) all suggest a lack of emotional reaction, which is similar to "desensitized," not its opposite. Hence, (d) Sensitive is the correct antonym.

questions.question: Identify the type of sentence: "Research has indicated that excessive consumption of violent media can increase aggressive thoughts and reduce empathy towards others."
questions.explanation: The sentence contains one independent clause: "Research has indicated" and one dependent (subordinate) clause introduced by "that": "that excessive consumption of violent media can increase aggressive thoughts and reduce empathy towards others." Since it has one main clause and one subordinate clause, it is classified as a complex sentence. It is not simple because it contains more than one clause, not compound because there are no two independent clauses joined by a conjunction, and not compound-complex because there is only one independent clause. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Are regular health check-ups for all the employees conducted by the company's medical team every single year?
options: Had the company's medical team conducted regular health check-ups for all the employees every single year?
Do the company's medical team conducted regular health check-ups for all the employees every single year?
Does the company's medical team conduct regular health check-ups for all the employees every single year?
Has the company's medical team conducted regular health check-ups for all the employees every single year?
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" verb ['are'] and the preposition "by" is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. The sentence is an interrogative sentence, and the active form will begin with a helping verb like: Do, does. In Passive voice, the format is: Am/Is/Are + Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case)? Thus, the format of the sentence which begins with "Do" or "Does" in Active voice is: Do/Does + Subject + V1 + Object? For example: Is therapy needed by him? (Passive) Does he need therapy? (Active) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (c): Does the company's medical team conduct regular health check-ups for all the employees every single year? Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Abstain
questions.explanation: Abstain means to deliberately avoid or refrain from something, especially something enjoyable; for example, "He chose to abstain from voting." Forego means to do without or give up voluntarily, making forego the correct synonym. Relish means to enjoy something greatly. Acquire means to obtain. Pardon means to forgive. None of these align with the meaning of "abstain." Hence, option a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. This exfoliating scrub can completely make you feel young.
questions.explanation: WORDMEANING RemissNot showing enough care and attention NascentBeginning to exist and evolve RejuvenateTo make somebody/something feel or look younger RegaliaThe symbols of royalty 'Rejuvenate' is the right choice for the underlined word. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Rapacious
questions.explanation: 'Rapacious' means 'Aggressively greedy or grasping.'. Options b) and d) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Satisfied' means 'Contented; pleased.'. Hence, option c) can also be rejected as it is the exact opposite. 'Apprehension' means 'Anxiety or fear that something bad or unpleasant will happen.'. 'Menial' means '(of work) not requiring much skill and lacking prestige.'. Hence, option a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Such conditions significantly impact vulnerable populations, including children, elderly individuals, and those with pre-existing health conditions. B. Homes often lack proper airflow, leading to increased temperatures and the accumulation of pollutants, affecting respiratory health. C. In rural India, extreme climatic conditions, especially during the summer months, create discomfort and health hazards due to inadequate ventilation. D. Introducing efficient fans can transform these living conditions, providing relief by enhancing air circulation and reducing indoor temperatures to create a healthier environment for all residents.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a coherent paragraph is CBAD. Sentence C comes first as it introduces the specific problem (extreme climatic conditions and inadequate ventilation) in rural India, which is discussed in all other sentences. Following the introduction of the problem in rural India, sentence B provides a general explanation of how inadequate airflow affects homes. After explaining how inadequate airflow affects homes, sentence A discusses who is most affected by these conditions. Sentence D comes at last as it concludes the paragraph by proposing a solution to the problem and suggests that introducing efficient fans can improve living conditions. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. P. regular maintenance of your vehicle Q. reducing the risk of unexpected breakdowns R. and costly repairs in the future S. ensures its longevity and reliability on the road,
questions.explanation: P is the starting point, indicating an action or process. S logically follows the first one, as it explains the outcome or benefit of regular maintenance. The word "ensures" implies a cause-and-effect relationship. Q fits after the previous phrase about longevity and reliability. If maintenance ensures longevity and reliability, then it follows that it also reduces the risk of unexpected breakdowns. R completes the logical flow by explaining another consequence of regular maintenance. Hence, (a) PSQR is the correct answer. The final sentence: Regular maintenance of your vehicle ensures its longevity and reliability on the road, reducing the risk of unexpected breakdowns and costly repairs in the future.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The botanist identified the tree as a type of sallow by its flexible branches.
options: After the long illness, her complexion was pale and sallow.
The goat willow is a common species of sallow.
A sallow is a low-growing willow tree, often found near water.
The basket weaver preferred to use the pliable twigs from a sallow.
questions.explanation: A homonym is a word that is spelled and pronounced the same as another word but has a different meaning. In the original sentence, "sallow" is a noun referring to a type of willow tree. In options (b), (c), and (d), "sallow" is used with this exact same meaning: a willow tree. In option (a), "sallow" is an adjective describing a person's complexion as being of an unhealthy yellow or pale brown color. This is a completely different meaning and part of speech, making it the correct homonym. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. She has been working / in that company / since five years / with great dedication.
questions.explanation: The phrase "since five years" is incorrect because "since" is used with a specific point in time (e.g., since 2018, since Monday). For a duration of time, we use "for" instead. In the given sentence, a duration of time is discussed and not a specific point in time; thus, 'for' should be used and not 'since'. Correct phrase: "for five years" Correct sentence: She has been working in that company for five years with great dedication. So, the error is in the part given in option (c). Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. The new policy aims at reducing the cost and to improve the quality of services.
questions.explanation: Parallelism requires both verbs to be in the same form. Here, "reducing" (gerund) is followed incorrectly by "to improve" (to-infinitive). The phrase "aims at" should be followed by a gerund only, so "reducing" is correct, and the to-infinitive "to improve" needs to be replaced with the gerund "improving". Correct: "reducing the cost and improving the quality of services." Correct sentence: The new policy aims at reducing the cost and improving the quality of services. So, the error is in the part given in option (d). Hence, option (d) is the answer. Note: Parallelism: In grammar, parallelism, also known as parallel structure or parallel construction, is a balance within one or more sentences of similar phrases or clauses that have the same grammatical structure. Given below is an example which shows a sentence in two forms (parallel in structure and lacking parallelism): Lacking parallelism: Prerna likes cooking, dancing and to sing. Parallel: Prerna likes cooking, dancing and singing. Gerund: Gerund is a verb form that functions as a noun, ending in -ing. A gerund can be either the subject or object of a sentence in English. Example: "Everybody enjoyed her dancing and singing at the party." The words 'dancing' and 'singing' are gerunds. Infinitive: The infinitive is the base form of a verb, often preceded by to. Consider the sentences given below: · To err is human. [like a noun, the infinitive 'to err' is the subject of the verb 'is'] · My father likes to cook. [Like a noun, the infinitive 'to cook' is the object of the verb 'likes'] · To vote in elections is our right. [The infinitive 'to vote', like a noun, is the subject of the verb 'is', but, like a verb, it also takes an object (elections)] · Radha went to the lake to fetch water. [The infinitive 'to fetch', like a noun, is the object of the verb 'went', but, like a verb, it also takes an object (water)] · The above sentences illustrate various ways in which infinitives are used. It can both act as a noun and a verb.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined word in the given sentence. I waited at the bus stop until two hours, but no bus came.
questions.explanation: The preposition "until" indicates the endpoint of a time period, not the duration itself. The correct expression here refers to duration, which takes a preposition. Option a) for is correct - it shows how long the action continued. Correct sentence: "I waited at the bus stop for two hours, but no bus came." Other options: b) since refers to a starting point in the past - incorrect. c) within suggests something happened before a limit - wrong meaning. d) by implies a deadline, which doesn't fit. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The IPS officer ordered his assistant to bring him the files on the Nostron family.
options: The IPS officer said to his assistant, "Bring me the files on the Nostron family."
The IPS officer asked his assistant, "Bring me the files on the Nostron family."
The IPS officer said to his assistant, "Please bring me the files on the Nostron family."
The IPS officer says to his assistant, "Bring me the files on the Nostron family."
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) The IPS officer said to his assistant, "Bring me the files on the Nostron family." Key points: ·Imperative Sentences in Direct Speech are used to demonstrate Request, Order, Command, etc. ·Imperative Sentences in Direct Speech begin with Principal verbs like Go, Make, Bring, etc. ·"To" is used before the Principal Verbs in Indirect Speech. ·For example: o John ordered his assistant to bring him a glass of water. (Indirect Speech) o John said to his assistant, "Bring a glass of water." (Direct Speech) ·The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech:Indirect SpeechDirect Speech orderedSaid to to bringBring himme Also, option (d) is incorrect because the reporting verb doesn't change, so the conversion of 'ordered' into 'says to' is incorrect. ·Therefore, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence: The polite customer said to the waiter, "Please bring me a glass of cold water and the menu card for the main course as soon as possible."
options: The polite customer ordered the waiter to bring him a glass of cold water and the menu card for the main course as soon as possible.
The polite customer told the waiter that he should bring him a glass of cold water and the menu card for the main course as soon as possible.
The polite customer requested the waiter to bring him a glass of cold water and the menu card for the main course as soon as possible.
The polite customer said to the waiter to please bring him a glass of cold water and the menu card for the main course as soon as possible.
questions.explanation: Imperative Sentences are used to demonstrate Request, Order, Command, etc. The reporting verbs like "said", "say", etc. along with words like "Please" are changed into appropriate verbs like requested. "To" is used before the Principal Verbs in the Indirect Speech. For example: John said to his assistant, "Bring me a glass of water." (Direct Speech) John ordered his assistant to bring him a glass of water. (Indirect Speech) The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: · said to and Please change to requested. · The imperative verb bring becomes to bring. · me changes to him. Therefore, the correct answer is: "The polite customer requested the waiter to bring him a glass of cold water and the menu card for the main course as soon as possible." Hence, option (c) is correct.
